MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-27-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT EDWARD SAVINO'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's Motion to Strike Punitive Damages as to Defendant Edward Savino is DENIED. Complaint sufficiently alleges malice, oppression and/or fraud as to Defendant Savino to sustain a request for punitive damages at the pleading stage. Defendant's Renewed Motion to Strike Punitive Damages is STRICKEN. A CCP 1008(b) is not appropriate, and even if it was there are still sufficient facts to support a request for punitive damages. MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Alexander J. Berline of Hanson Bridgett LLP (415-995-5035) appears telephonically as counsel for plaintiffs Norma Mulligan, Patrick Mulligan, 2936-2938 Lyon Street Home Owners

Attorney, Amy E. Hammond of Grotefeld Hoffmann Schleiter et al LLP (415-344-9670) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party, Granite State Insurance Company in related action CGC-15-548037

Attorney, Amanda Stevens of Schroeder Loscotoff (916-438-8300) appears telephonically for plaintiff, Nevada Capital Insurance Company

Attorney, Bryan E. Stofferahn of Bremer Whyte Brown & O'Meara LLP (510-540-4881) appears as counsel for defendant AV-LIL Investments LLC in related action CGC-16-551569

The cause is argued and submitted by all sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court modifies and confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT MARK BUDAK, AV-LIL INVESTMENTS, LLC Joinder In Motion To Consolidate Related Cases is DENIED without prejudice to Granite State Insurance Company filing a motion to intervene in the Mulligan action if it so wishes. The Mulligan Action involves many factual allegations beyond the fire, as well as different parties, causes of action and requested remedies. My ruling is without prejudice to a motion to consolidate the other five pending actions, which are more closely related. Moreover, plaintiffs in the Mulligan Action express willingness to coordinate discovery with the other cases - a step I encourage. Prevailing party to submit a [proposed] written order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
